section.heading: Notification of certain individuals regarding options for enrollment under Medicare part B

paragraph.chapeau: As soon as practicable, the Secretary of Defense shall notify each individual described in subsection (b)—

subparagraph.content: that the individual is no longer eligible for health care benefits under the TRICARE program under this chapter; and
of options available for enrollment of the individual in the supplementary medical insurance program under part B of title XVIII of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395j et seq.).

paragraph.chapeau: In carrying out this subsection, the Secretary of Defense shall—

subparagraph.content: establish procedures for identifying individuals described in subsection (b); and
consult with the Secretary of Health and Human Services to accurately identify and notify such individuals.

subsection.heading: Individuals Described.—
subsection.chapeau: An individual described in this subsection is an individual who is—

paragraph.content: entitled to benefits under part A of title XVIII of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395c) under section 226(b) or section 226A of such Act (42 U.S.C. 426(b) and 426–1); and
eligible to enroll in the supplementary medical insurance program under part B of such title (42 U.S.C. 1395j et seq.).

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 111–84, div. A, title VII, § 707(a), Oct. 28, 2009, 123 Stat. 2376.)

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: The Social Security Act, referred to in subsecs. (a)(1)(B) and (b)(2), (3), is act Aug. 14, 1935, ch. 531, 49 Stat. 620. Parts A and B of title XVIII of the Act are classified generally to parts A (§ 1395c et seq.) and B (§ 1395j et seq.), respectively, of subchapter XVIII of chapter 7 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see section 1305 of Title 42 and Tables.
